entries.name: Gregory Paradis
body: Dr. Gregory Paradis leads FRESH in the UBC Faculty of Forestry & Environmental Stewardship.
His research applies operations research, mathematical optimization, data science, and forest-sector systems modelling to complex forest management problems.
He uses a systems approach to model interactions among forest ecosystems, industrial supply chains, public policy, ecosystem services, and society.

pages.title: Postdocs and Researchers
pages.summary: Research associates and staff researchers connected to FRESH.

entries.name: Kathleen Coupland
body: Dr. Kathleen Coupland is a forest carbon modeller and registered professional forester whose work supports sustainable forest management and environmental conservation.
Her background combines forestry education, sustainable forest management, and practical knowledge of forest ecosystems and their role in climate-change mitigation.
In addition to her FRESH research role, Kathleen works part-time as coordinator of the Master of Geomatics for Environmental Management program.

pages.title: Graduate Students
pages.summary: Current and incoming master's students and supervised program students.

entries.name: Yunhao (Davis) Xu
body: Yunhao (Davis) Xu is a MASc student in FRESH with a forest operations background and a research interest in mathematical modelling for forest operations and planning.
His current thesis work develops forest estate modelling and decision-support workflows for post-wildfire salvage planning.
Before joining FRESH, Davis completed a B.Sc. in the UBC Faculty of Forestry and worked as a research assistant on field data collection.

entries.name: Jamie Iversen
body: Jamie Iversen is an MSc student in FRESH and a registered forester-in-training in British Columbia.
His research focuses on risk-aware forest estate modelling for wildfire mitigation, linking fuel-treatment planning, timber supply analysis, and landscape-level decision support.
Jamie's past work as a wildland firefighter helped motivate his interest in wildfire fuel treatments and operationally useful planning tools.

entries.name: Thomas Cooper
body: Thomas Cooper is an incoming master's student in FRESH.
His developing MSc direction connects to FRESH interests in quantitative forest-sector modelling, open research tools, and decision support.

entries.name: Kailey
body: Kailey is a TRANSFOR-M program student supervised by Gregory Paradis.
Her work connects to FRESH interests in drone seeding, microsite selection, and decision support for reforestation and restoration.

pages.title: Visiting Scholars
pages.summary: FRESH occasionally hosts visiting scholars and collaborators connected to forest-sector modelling, ecosystem services, and decision-support research.

pages.title: Former FRESHies
pages.summary: Former FRESH members and collaborators are part of the lab's research record; this page focuses on public, current, and approved alumni information.

entries.name: Rosalia Jaffray
body: Rosalia Jaffray completed MASc work in FRESH on forest harvesting operational planning, operations research, and open-source machine scheduling.
Her thesis developed and evaluated FHOPS, the Forest Harvesting Operations Planning System, as a reproducible framework for forest operations planning experiments.
Rosalia's background combines forest operations, engineering, harvesting systems, and practical interest in decision-support tools for forest operations.

entries.name: Yancun (Walter) Yan
body: Yancun (Walter) Yan completed MSc work on mathematical modelling to simulate and optimize climate-change mitigation potential in British Columbia's forest sector.
His work linked forest ecosystem carbon, harvested wood products, timber supply, and optimization methods in an integrated modelling framework.
Walter's background includes forestry, ecology, programming, mathematical modelling, and geospatial analysis.

entries.name: Jinming (Jimmy) Ke
body: Jinming (Jimmy) Ke completed MSc work on system-level climate impact assessment for forest management, fertilization, harvested wood products, and substitution pathways.

His thesis addressed the connection between forest carbon management and product carbon management by tracking carbon flows from forest management through harvested wood product use and eventual emissions.
Jimmy's modelling work used transparent Python workflows to support reproducible assessment of climate-smart forest management scenarios.

entries.name: Bridget Guo
body: Bridget Guo was an undergraduate intern in FRESH working on ecosystem modelling and statistical analysis.
They completed a B.Sc. in Natural Resources Conservation and brought experience in coding, data analysis, and data manipulation from a co-op term with the Ministry of Forests.
Bridget contributed to FRESH modelling work connected to hemlock dwarf mistletoe spread.
